join_order.build: qualifying_orders_with_sum_qty(streamed from lineitem grouped by contiguous l_orderkey runs, HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 300)
join_order.notes: Carry forward the already-computed per-order sum_qty and resolve each surviving order through orders_pk_dense.
Resolve o_custkey to customer row ids via dense direct addressing; no second lineitem join is needed.

filters.predicate: streamed GROUP BY l_orderkey with HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 300

data_structures.aggregation: streamed_sorted_group_by_on_lineitem; reuse carried sum_qty as final aggregate

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current logical plan recomputes the outer SUM(l_quantity) by revisiting lineitem postings even though the inner HAVING pass already computes the exact per-order aggregate for the same l_orderkey groups, so the remaining hot-path work includes unnecessary second-pass lineitem accesses and index traffic for only 624 surviving orders. Strategy category A: carry sum_qty forward from the initial streamed sorted aggregation, eliminate the outer lineitem/postings fetch entirely, keep only dense direct-array lookups into orders and customer, and run the scan with bandwidth-efficient moderate parallelism instead of a fixed 64-thread fanout.
